steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The passage explicitly poses its central question: "What explains its rise in popularity?" and then answers it: y'all fills a gap because many English varieties lack a dedicated plural "you." The correct choice must match the question the passage actually answers.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'How many other plural versions of the pronoun "you" are there in English, besides "y'all"?': the passage never lists or counts other plural pronouns. ✗
  • Choice B — 'When was the first recorded use of the pronoun "y'all" in the English language?': the passage mentions regions of use, not historical origin dates. ✗
  • Choice C — 'Is "y'all" commonly used in English-speaking regions of the world besides the US?': the passage only discusses use within the US. ✗
  • Choice D — 'Why has the pronoun "y'all" become more widely used in the US?': this is exactly what the passage answers — y'all spread because it uniquely fills the gap for a plural "you." ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
